frame_label: Dress Code: Traditional = Respectable, Other = Questionable
generated_comment: Divya: Honestly, for family functions I still feel saree or at least a proper salwar looks much more decent. These very western, short outfits just invite unnecessary attention, and then people act surprised when boys stare.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it links a woman's clothing with decency and subtly blames harassment on 'western' dress. It encodes the traditional-equals-respectable framing and the clothing-harassment link.
original_frame: Workplace: Menstrual and Maternity Leave Bias
persona_text: You are Divya. Age 35. A senior software engineer in Bengaluru. You are married with one child. You are venting to your husband at home after dinner. Your skip level manager pulled you into a meeting and told you that if you plan to have another child you should think carefully about whether this is the right time to take on a high visibility project. You are telling your husband what was said to you.
